session.user:
FIRST check docs/learned/index.md for existing documentation on this topic. Read relevant docs before exploring raw files. Then explore codebase as necessary.
I need to understand the patterns used in the erk codebase for implementing a new `erk stack sync` command. Specifically:
1. Read `src/erk/cli/commands/stack/list_cmd.py` to understand an existing stack command pattern 2. Read `src/erk/cli/graphite_command.py` to understand GraphiteCommand 3. Read the git branch_ops ABC to find: `is_branch_diverged_from_remote`, `branch_exists_on_remote`, `update_local_ref`, `get_branch_head`, `checkout_branch` — find the file and read the relevant signatures 4. Read the git remote_ops ABC to find: `fetch_prune`, `pull_rebase` — find the file and read relevant signatures 5. Read the git worktree ABC to find: `is_branch_checked_out` — find the file and read relevant signatures 6. Read the git rebase_ops ABC to find: `is_rebase_in_progress`, `rebase_abort` 7. Find `PullRebaseResult`, `PullRebaseError`, `BranchDivergence` types 8. Find `graphite_branch_ops.retrack_branch` — the graphite branch ops ABC 9. Look at how `ErkContext` is structured — what attributes it has for git, graphite, branch_manager etc. 10. Look at `tests/commands/stack/test_list_cmd.py` for test patterns 11. Look at `tests/commands/pr/test_diverge_fix.py` for diverge-fix test patterns
Return the key API signatures, type definitions, and patterns I need to implement `erk stack sync`.
session.assistant: I'll help you understand the patterns for implementing `erk stack sync`. Let me start by checking the documentation, then systematically explore the codebase.

1→<!-- AUTO-GENERATED FILE - DO NOT EDIT DIRECTLY --> 2→<!-- Edit source frontmatter, then run 'erk docs sync' to regenerate. --> 3→ 4→# Agent Documentation 5→ 6→Before starting work, scan the read-when conditions below. 7→If your current task matches, read the linked document **before writing code**. 8→ 9→## Categories 10→ 11→- [architecture/](architecture/) — Explore when working on core patterns (dry-run, gateways, subprocess, shell integration). Add docs here for cross-cutting technical patterns. 12→- [capabilities/](capabilities/) 13→- [changelog/](changelog/) 14→- [checklists/](checklists/) 15→- [ci/](ci/) 16→- [claude-code/](claude-code/) 17→- [cli/](cli/) — Explore when building CLI commands or output formatting. Add docs here for Click patterns and terminal UX. 18→- [commands/](commands/) — Explore when creating or optimizing slash commands. Add docs here for command authoring patterns. 19→- [config/](config/) 20→- [configuration/](configuration/) 21→- [documentation/](documentation/) — Explore when structuring or writing documentation. Add docs here for documentation methodology and best practices. 22→- [erk/](erk/) — Explore when working with erk-specific workflows (worktrees, PR sync, Graphite). Add docs here for erk user-facing features. 23→- [erk-dev/](erk-dev/) 24→- [gateway/](gateway/) 25→- [hooks/](hooks/) — Explore when creating or debugging hooks. Add docs here for hook development patterns. 26→- [integrations/](integrations/) 27→- [objectives/](objectives/) 28→- [planning/](planning/) — Explore when working with plans, .erk/impl-context/ folders, or agent delegation. Add docs here for planning workflow patterns. 29→- [pr-operations/](pr-operations/) 30→- [refactoring/](refactoring/)

1→"""Custom Click command classes that require Graphite integration. 2→ 3→This module provides declarative command classes that automatically: 4→1. Check Graphite availability before command execution 5→2. Are hidden from help output when Graphite is unavailable 6→ 7→Usage: 8→ @click.command("list", cls=GraphiteCommand) 9→ def list_stack(ctx: ErkContext) -> None: 10→ # No need for Ensure.graphite_available(ctx) - handled by GraphiteCommand 11→ ... 12→ 13→ @click.command("up", cls=GraphiteCommandWithHiddenOptions) 14→ @script_option 15→ def up_cmd(ctx: ErkContext, script: bool) -> None: 16→ # Combines Graphite check with hidden options support 17→ ... 18→ 19→ @click.group("stack", cls=GraphiteGroup) 20→ def stack_group() -> None: 21→ # Entire group hidden when Graphite unavailable 22→ ... 23→""" 24→ 25→from typing import Any 26→ 27→import click 28→ 29→from erk.cli.ensure import Ensure 30→from erk.cli.help_formatter import CommandWithHiddenOptions

1→"""Discriminated union types for Git branch operations. 2→ 3→BranchCreated | BranchAlreadyExists follows the NonIdealState pattern 4→established by PushResult | PushError in remote_ops/types.py. 5→""" 6→ 7→from dataclasses import dataclass 8→ 9→ 10→@dataclass(frozen=True) 11→class BranchCreated: 12→ """Success result from creating a branch.""" 13→ 14→ 15→@dataclass(frozen=True) 16→class BranchAlreadyExists: 17→ """Error: branch already exists. Implements NonIdealState.""" 18→ 19→ branch_name: str 20→ message: str 21→ 22→ @property 23→ def error_type(self) -> str: 24→ return "branch-already-exists" 25→

1→"""Abstract base class for Git rebase operations. 2→ 3→This sub-gateway extracts rebase operations from the main Git gateway, 4→including rebase_onto, rebase_continue, rebase_abort, and is_rebase_in_progress. 5→""" 6→ 7→from __future__ import annotations 8→ 9→from abc import ABC, abstractmethod 10→from pathlib import Path 11→from typing import TYPE_CHECKING 12→ 13→if TYPE_CHECKING: 14→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.abc import RebaseResult 15→ 16→ 17→class GitRebaseOps(ABC): 18→ """Abstract interface for Git rebase operations. 19→ 20→ This interface contains both mutation and query operations for rebasing. 21→ All implementations (real, fake, dry-run, printing) must implement this interface. 22→ """ 23→ 24→ # ============================================================================ 25→ # Mutation Operations 26→ # ============================================================================ 27→ 28→ @abstractmethod 29→ def rebase_onto(self, cwd: Path, target_ref: str) -> RebaseResult: 30→ """Rebase the current branch onto a target ref.

53→ subprocess.CalledProcessError: If continue fails (e.g., unresolved conflicts) 68→ subprocess.CalledProcessError: If no rebase is in progress

1→"""High-level git operations interface. 2→ 3→This module provides a clean abstraction over git subprocess calls, making the 4→codebase more testable and maintainable. 5→ 6→Architecture: 7→- Git: Abstract base class defining the interface 8→- RealGit: Production implementation using subprocess 9→- Standalone functions: Convenience wrappers delegating to module singleton 10→""" 11→ 12→from __future__ import annotations 13→ 14→from abc import ABC, abstractmethod 15→from dataclasses import dataclass 16→from pathlib import Path 17→from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, NamedTuple 18→ 19→if TYPE_CHECKING: 20→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.analysis_ops.abc import GitAnalysisOps 21→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.branch_ops.abc import GitBranchOps 22→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.commit_ops.abc import GitCommitOps 23→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.config_ops.abc import GitConfigOps 24→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.rebase_ops.abc import GitRebaseOps 25→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.remote_ops.abc import GitRemoteOps 26→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.repo_ops.abc import GitRepoOps 27→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.status_ops.abc import GitStatusOps 28→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.tag_ops.abc import GitTagOps 29→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.worktree.abc import Worktree 30→

1→"""Abstract base class for Graphite branch operations. 2→ 3→This sub-gateway extracts branch mutation operations from the main Graphite gateway, 4→making BranchManager the enforced abstraction for branch mutations. 5→""" 6→ 7→from __future__ import annotations 8→ 9→from abc import ABC, abstractmethod 10→from pathlib import Path 11→ 12→ 13→class GraphiteBranchOps(ABC): 14→ """Abstract interface for Graphite branch mutation operations. 15→ 16→ This interface contains ONLY mutation operations that modify branch state. 17→ Query operations (get_all_branches, get_branch_stack, etc.) remain on the 18→ main Graphite ABC for convenience. 19→ 20→ All implementations (real, fake, dry-run, printing) must implement this interface. 21→ """ 22→ 23→ @abstractmethod 24→ def track_branch(self, cwd: Path, branch_name: str, parent_branch: str) -> None: 25→ """Track a branch with Graphite. 26→ 27→ Uses `gt track` to register a branch in Graphite's cache. This is needed 28→ when branches are created with direct git operations (git branch) instead 29→ of gt create. 30→

1→"""Application context with dependency injection. 2→ 3→This module provides factory functions for erk CLI context creation. 4→The unified ErkContext dataclass is defined in erk_shared.context and 5→re-exported here for backwards compatibility. 6→""" 7→ 8→from __future__ import annotations 9→ 10→import shutil 11→from collections.abc import MutableMapping 12→from pathlib import Path 13→from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any, cast 14→ 15→import click 16→import tomlkit 17→ 18→# Re-export types from erk_shared.context and erk.artifacts.paths 19→from erk.artifacts.paths import ErkPackageInfo as ErkPackageInfo 20→from erk.cli.config import load_config, load_local_config, merge_configs_with_local 21→from erk.core.codex_prompt_executor import CodexCliPromptExecutor 22→from erk.core.completion import RealCompletion 23→from erk.core.prompt_executor import ClaudeCliPromptExecutor 24→from erk.core.repo_discovery import discover_repo_or_sentinel, ensure_erk_metadata_dir 25→from erk.core.script_writer import RealScriptWriter 26→from erk.core.services.objective_list_service import RealObjectiveListService 27→from erk.core.services.plan_list_service import PlannedPRPlanListService 28→from erk.core.shell import RealShell 29→ 30→# Re-export ErkContext from erk_shared for isinstance() compatibility

1→"""Move branches between worktrees with explicit source specification.""" 2→ 3→from pathlib import Path 4→ 5→import click 6→ 7→from erk.cli.commands.completions import complete_worktree_names 8→from erk.cli.core import discover_repo_context, worktree_path_for 9→from erk.cli.ensure import Ensure 10→from erk.core.context import ErkContext 11→from erk.core.repo_discovery import ensure_erk_metadata_dir 12→from erk.core.worktree_utils import ( 13→ MoveOperationType, 14→ determine_move_operation, 15→ find_worktree_containing_path, 16→ find_worktree_with_branch, 17→ get_worktree_branch, 18→) 19→from erk_shared.output.output import user_output 20→ 21→ 22→def _resolve_current_worktree(ctx: ErkContext, repo_root: Path) -> Path: 23→ """Find worktree containing current directory. 24→ 25→ Raises SystemExit if not in a git repository or not in any worktree. 26→ """ 27→ Ensure.not_none(ctx.git.repo.get_git_common_dir(ctx.cwd), "Not in a git repository") 28→ 29→ cwd = ctx.cwd.resolve() 30→ worktrees = ctx.git.worktree.list_worktrees(repo_root)

1→"""Abstract base class for BranchManager operations.""" 2→ 3→from __future__ import annotations 4→ 5→from abc import ABC, abstractmethod 6→from pathlib import Path 7→ 8→from erk_shared.gateway.branch_manager.types import PrInfo, SubmitBranchError, SubmitBranchResult 9→from erk_shared.gateway.git.branch_ops.types import BranchAlreadyExists, BranchCreated 10→ 11→ 12→class BranchManager(ABC): 13→ """Dual-mode interface for branch operations. 14→ 15→ Provides consistent interface regardless of whether 16→ Graphite or plain Git is being used. This abstraction 17→ allows the statusline (and other consumers) to work 18→ transparently with both Graphite-managed and plain Git 19→ repositories. 20→ 21→ Key operations: 22→ - get_pr_for_branch: Get PR info from Graphite cache (fast) or GitHub API (fallback) 23→ - create_branch: Create branch via gt create (Graphite) or git branch (Git) 24→ """ 25→ 26→ @abstractmethod 27→ def get_pr_for_branch(self, repo_root: Path, branch: str) -> PrInfo | None: 28→ """Get PR info for a branch. 29→ 30→ For Graphite: Reads from .graphite_pr_info cache (fast, no network).
